name: Portable Sheds
description:
Portable sheds are storage and shelter structures built to be moved — taken apart, transported, and re-erected on a new site instead of demolished and rebuilt. SAMAN manufactures them as a bolt-together steel-and-wood unit; the configuration on this page is 40 × 30 × 14 ft (1,200 sq ft) at ₹7,55,000 (regular ₹7,65,000, SKU SP-CC-40-2024), fabricated at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities and delivered across India. If your operation shifts location — a project site that finishes, a yard that relocates, a lease that ends — this is the shed that goes with you.

What Makes a Shed Portable — and Can You Move It After It's Built?

SAMAN's signature steel-grey portable shed sited on an industrial yard before any relocation.

A portable shed is a structure assembled from bolted steel framing and wood panels rather than poured concrete, so it can be unbolted, loaded onto a truck, and reassembled elsewhere. That bolt-together construction — not its size — is what makes it portable. A SAMAN portable shed is built to be relocated several times across its working life.

How Relocating a Portable Shed Actually Works

A portable shed coming apart for relocation — panels off, bolted frame breaking into sections.

Relocating a portable shed is a planned three-step job, not an improvised lift. First, the unit is dismantled: roof and wall panels come off, then the bolted steel frame is broken down into transportable sections — the same connections that held it together on the first site. Second, the sections and panels are loaded and trucked to the new location. Third, the frame is re-erected on the prepared spot and the panels refitted. Because nothing is welded in place or cast into concrete, the same movable shed can be taken down and put back up several times without losing structural integrity. Each move does mean checking bolts, seals, and panel fit on reassembly — relocation is repeatable, but it is a proper site task with a crew, not a one-person lift.

Siting a Portable Shed: Ground Prep, Levelling & Foundation

A portable shed seated on a prepared, level base with footing pads — no deep foundation needed.

Because a portable shed carries its own bolted steel frame, it does not need the deep RCC foundation a permanent building does — but it does need a firm, level base to sit on. On most project sites a compacted, levelled pad or an existing concrete floor is enough; for softer ground or longer placements, simple footing pads under the frame columns keep it stable and off wet soil. The one non-negotiable is level: an out-of-level base twists the frame and stops doors and panels from seating correctly. Portable site sheds work best when the ground is prepared before delivery, so the crew can set the unit down and bolt it up the same day rather than waiting on civil work. You arrange the base; we handle the structure.

When a Portable Shed Beats a Fixed Storage Shed

A portable shed earning its place on a temporary highway project — the case for movable over fixed.

The choice between a portable shed and a permanent one comes down to a single question: will the structure stay where you first put it? If the location is settled and the shed's job is to hold goods on one spot for years, a fixed, enclosed shed that stays put is the more economical build — it is engineered to stand, not to travel. A portable shed earns its place when the answer is temporary or uncertain: a construction site that wraps up in eighteen months, a leased yard, a storage need that follows seasonal work, or equipment that must shift between two locations. Even large portable sheds at this 1,200 sq ft scale are designed around movability — which is why the frame is bolted rather than welded and the panels are demountable. You pay a little for relocatability; you get back the freedom to move the asset instead of abandoning it.

What ₹7,55,000 Covers for the 40×30×14 ft Portable Shed

Inside the build — bolted steel frame and wood infill, the construction that lets the shed re-erect.

The 40 × 30 × 14 ft portable shed is priced at ₹7,55,000 (regular ₹7,65,000) for 1,200 sq ft of covered space. That figure covers the bolted structural steel frame, the wood wall and roof infill, the demountable panels, delivery, and first-site assembly. The steel-and-wood combination is deliberate for a unit meant to move: the steel frame gives the structure something to bolt and re-bolt to without fatigue, while the wood panels are light enough to lift off and refit by hand rather than needing a crane on every move. If your decision is really about steel grade and coating rather than whether the shed moves, that is a different question — our steel grade and coating options cover it. Priced separately here is anything tied to how you use or move the unit: a different footprint, extra doors for vehicle or equipment access, added ventilation, or reinforced flooring for heavier portable utility sheds carrying machinery. Tell us the size and what goes inside, and the quote is built to that rather than estimated per square foot.

Ordering a Portable Shed from SAMAN

Buying is direct from the manufacturer: send your size, site location, and how often the shed will move, and we return a quote, a layout drawing, and a lead time. This portable shed is part of SAMAN's industrial shed range, built and dispatched from our Bangalore and Greater Noida units to sites across India — so you are dealing with the people who fabricate it, not a reseller chain. If what you actually need is a unit people will work or live inside rather than a storage-and-shelter structure, a habitable portable cabin is the better starting point; sheds are not fitted out for occupancy.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is it cheaper to buy a portable shed or build a permanent one?

For a structure you will keep on one site indefinitely, a permanent build is usually cheaper over its life. A portable shed wins on total cost when the alternative is building, demolishing, and rebuilding as you relocate — you move the ₹7,55,000 asset instead of writing it off. The fewer years a structure stays on one spot, the stronger the case for portable.

How many times can a portable shed be moved?

There is no fixed limit. Because the frame is bolted and the panels are demountable, a SAMAN portable shed can be relocated repeatedly across its working life. Each move means dismantling, transport, and re-erecting on a level base, with bolts and seals checked on reassembly. Practical lifespan depends far more on careful handling during each move than on the number of moves.

Does a portable shed need a concrete foundation?

No deep RCC foundation is required. A portable shed needs a firm, level base — a compacted pad, an existing concrete floor, or footing pads under the frame columns for softer ground. Getting the base level before delivery matters most; it lets the crew set down and bolt up the shed in a single visit.

Get a Portable Shed Quote

Send your size, site location, and how often the shed needs to move — we'll confirm an itemised price and lead time.
Bangalore — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
short_description:
SAMAN Portable Shed
40×30×14 ft · Built to relocate between sites

Bolt-together steel-and-wood shed for storage and shelter — dismantles, moves, and re-erects on a new site. No permanent foundation needed.

Size / Capacity
40×30×14 ft · 1,200 sq ft
Frame / Panel
Steel frame · Wood panel
Price / Delivery
₹7,55,000 · Pan-India
FloorLoad / Relocations
Level base · Multi-move
SKU SP-CC-40-2024
Get a free quote
